name: Distinctive frontend design (anti-generic)
description: A reusable, paste-friendly checklist for producing distinctive, production-grade frontend UI (typography, tokens, motion, backgrounds) and avoiding generic AI aesthetics.

metadata.intent: Guide frontend UI generation toward distinctive, cohesive aesthetics while preserving usability and accessibility

conventions: Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision; minimalism needs precision, maximalism needs intentional structure.

phases.title: Commit to an aesthetic direction before coding

steps.description: State purpose + audience + tone. Pick a bold direction: brutalist, editorial, retro-futurist, luxury, playful, industrial, organic, etc. Define what makes it unforgettable (one memorable hook).
Typography: pick distinctive fonts and use them decisively (display + body). Use high-contrast pairings (serif+geo sans, display+mono). Avoid converging on the same default fonts across builds.
Color & theme: commit to a cohesive palette; implement tokens (CSS vars) for backgrounds, surfaces, borders, text, accents. Dominant base + sharp accents beats timid even distribution.
Motion: prefer one high-impact orchestrated moment (page load / staggered reveals) over scattered micro-animations. Respect reduced-motion; avoid motion that blocks task completion.
Backgrounds: add depth/atmosphere (layered gradients, subtle texture/noise, patterns) instead of flat solid color defaults. Keep readability high and contrast safe.

Anti-generic checks: reject (1) Inter/Roboto defaults, (2) clichéd purple gradients, (3) predictable hero+3-cards layout, (4) generic shadcn clone without brand tone. Replace with intentional, context-specific design choices.
validation_checks: pi validate (for a11y + UX semantic checks)
